behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all relevant file paths (lib.rs, v8.rs, ffi.h, ffi.c++, AGENTS.md, README.md, rust-review-checklist.md), describes the need for has_self detection, Member::StaticMethod, Function type, function_template_get_function, and documentation updates. The specific string literals like 'has_self', 'Member::StaticMethod', 'pub struct Function;', 'as_local_function', 'function_template_get_function', 'From<Local<\'a, Function>>' are implied by the behavioral description though not all

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the problem (no static method support) and expected behavior (detect receiver, register on constructor vs prototype, update FFI layer and docs) but does not specify exact code changes, line numbers, or literal replacement text. A developer must still design the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: 'has_self' variable name (test_jsg_method_static_detection), 'if has_self' exact string (same test), 'Member::StaticMethod' exact enum variant name (test_static_member_variant), 'pub struct Function;' exact declaration (test_function_type_and_ffi), 'as_local_function' exact method name, 'From<Local<\'a, Function>>' exact trait impl syntax. An alternative correct implementation using e.g. 'is_static', 'is_instance_method', 'Member::Static', or

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost every test is purely text-matching: reading file contents and checking for substring presence (e.g., 'assert "has_self" in lib_rs', 'assert "Member::StaticMethod" in lib_rs'). The only execution-based tests are rustfmt syntax checks (test_rustfmt_*). No test actually compiles the macro, runs it, or verifies runtime behavior. This is overwhelmingly 'assert foo in open(path).read()' pattern.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p (fail-to-pass) tests like test_jsg_method_static_detection and test_static_member_variant can be satisfied by simply inserting comment strings like '// has_self', '// if has_self', '// Member::StaticMethod' into the file without implementing any actual functionality. No compilation or execution is tested for these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires: (1) macro detection of receiver - tested via test_jsg_method_static_detection, (2) static member generation - tested via test_static_member_variant, (3) FFI changes - tested via test_function_type_and_ffi and test_ffi_cpp_implements_get_function, (4) documentation updates - tested via test_agents_md, test_readme, test_review_checklist. All explicitly required behaviors are covered.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names and docstrings explaining what each test checks. They are logically grouped by concern (syntax, macro logic, FFI, documentation).

structured_data_schema.reason: This task produces code changes and documentation updates, not structured data outputs like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands in the instruction and tests appear correct and consistent with the repository structure.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the files to modify which is appropriate for the task scope. It describes what needs to change at an architectural level without specifying exact line numbers or code snippets to insert.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is not an agentmd-edits task; it involves code and documentation changes across multiple files.
